title: Intensely Peppery Spanish Pork Shoulder Fabada Asturiana
description: A traditional Spanish dish from the Andalusian region, featuring a tender and flavorful pork shoulder in a rich and spicy broth, served in individual ramekins.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together the shio cure, black peppercorns, and smoked paprika.
Rub the cure mixture all over the pork shoulder, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, then remove it from the pot and set it aside.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth and water to the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the pork shoulder to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven, where it will braise for 2 1/2 hours.
After 2 1/2 hours, remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Skim any excess fat from the surface of the broth, then bring it to a simmer.
Reduce the broth until it has thickened into a rich and syrupy glaze, about 10 minutes.
Slice the pork shoulder into thin strips and divide it among individual ramekins.
Spoon the glaze over the top of each ramekin, then serve immediately.
